params.description: Sticker sheet appears showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: In the Warmup, you saw equal groups lined up into a rectangle. Check out this sticker sheet. The stickers aren't bunched up or scattered around. They're lined up. Every sticker has its own spot. Here's what I notice.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: See how they're lined up? They go across, and they go up and down. Everything lines up into a rectangle.

params.description: System briefly outlines the full rectangle shape around the sticker sheet.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers. Rectangle outline visible around the array.

beats.text: When objects are arranged like this, lined up in a rectangle with every spot filled, that's called an array.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in rectangular arrangement.

params.description: 3×4 array of stickers appears. 3 rows, 4 columns. Neutral state, no highlights.

beats.text: Here's what we call the parts you're looking at. Watch.

params.description: Row 1 (top horizontal line of 4 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: See this line going across? That's a row. Rows go across, left to right.

params.description: Row 2 highlights, then row 3 highlights sequentially.

beats.text: Row 1, row 2, row 3. Three rows going across.

params.description: All row highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.
Column 1 (left vertical line of 3 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: Now this direction. See this line going up and down? That's a column. Columns go up and down.

params.description: Column 2, column 3, column 4 highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Column 1, column 2, column 3, column 4. Four columns, all up and down.

params.description: All column highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.

beats.text: Think about the difference. Rows go across. Columns go up and down. How many rows going across does this array have?

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.

beats.text: How many rows? Rows go across.
Right. 3 rows going across. So this array has 3 rows and 4 columns. An array is equal groups organized into rows and columns.

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.
3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. multiple_choice tool active.

params.description: 2×6 dot array appears. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Here's a new array, this time with dots. How many rows does this one have? Rows go across.
How many rows? (Rows go across.)
That's right. 2 rows. The lines going across.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.
2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Now how many columns? Columns go up and down.
How many columns? (Columns go up and down.)
Yes. 6 columns. The lines going up and down. So this array has 2 rows and 6 columns.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.
2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

params.description: 3×5 dot array appears. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder appears in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available below.

beats.text: This array has 3 rows. Each row has 5 dots. We can say: 3 rows of 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially: row 1 (5 dots), row 2 (5 dots), row 3 (5 dots).

beats.text: Here's how that turns into a multiplication expression.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. Rows highlighted sequentially, animation complete.
Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. The first number tells us how many rows. That's 3.

params.description: 3 tile places into first slot of Equation Builder.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows [3] × [___]. First slot filled with 3.

beats.text: The second number tells us how many in each row. That's 5.

params.description: 5 tile places into second slot. Expression shows 3 × 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. That's just like saying 3 groups of 5. And you know equal groups means multiplication. So 3 rows of 5 becomes 3 times 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: Now here's a new array.

params.description: Equation Builder clears. Expression returns to [___] × [___].
3×5 array removed from screen.
New 4×2 dot array appears. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.
Equation Builder cleared, showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: Build the expression. First number is how many rows. Second is how many in each row.
Build the expression for this array.
Right. 4 times 2. That's 4 rows of 2. The words match the expression.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.
Equation Builder shows 4 × 2. Both slots filled correctly.
4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots. place_tile tool active.
Equation Builder showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder appears in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication symbol between them. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10.

beats.text: One more. Check out this array and build the expression for the rows.
Build the row expression for this array.

validator.description: Student placed 2 × 5, correct expression for rows

beats.text: Right. 2 times 5. That's 2 rows of 5. You've got it.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder showing completed expression 2 × 5.
2×5 dot array. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder in expression mode with two empty slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette available.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears on screen. 2 rows, 5 columns of dots.

beats.text: You've been describing arrays by rows, those lines going across. But here's the thing about arrays. There's another way to describe them.

params.description: Rows briefly highlight then fade.
Columns brighten and highlight.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array. Columns highlighted.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears.
Equation Builder appears in expression mode with two blank slots.

beats.text: You described this array by rows: 2 rows of 5. That gave us 2 times 5.

params.description: Rows highlight briefly in sequence.
Expression 2 × 5 appears in Equation Builder.
Expression 2 × 5 fades out.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: Here's something interesting -- watch what happens if I look at the columns instead.

params.description: Columns highlight sequentially: column 1, then column 2, column 3, column 4, column 5.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array with columns highlighted sequentially.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: I see 5 columns. Each one has 2 dots. So I can also say this is 5 columns of 2.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: 5 columns of 2 becomes 5 times 2. First number tells me how many columns. Second number tells me how many in each column.

params.description: Expression 5 × 2 appears in Equation Builder.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 5 × 2.

beats.text: Same array. Different way to describe it, which gives us a different expression. 2 rows of 5 was 2 times 5. 5 columns of 2 is 5 times 2. Both work for this array.

params.description: Both expressions display briefly: 2 × 5 and 5 × 2 with array between them.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing both expressions 2 × 5 and 5 × 2.

beats.text: Here's what matters: how you describe the array is what you write. What you say becomes your expression. Let's try one.

params.description: 2×5 array removed.
Equation Builder changes to word description mode: ___ columns of ___.
4×3 dot array appears.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in word mode with two blank slots: ___ columns of ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: This array has 4 rows of 3. But what if we describe it by columns? Count the columns going up and down. Then count how many dots are in each column.
Describe this array by columns.

params.description: 3 columns of 4 appears in word description.

beats.text: Right. 3 columns of 4. Now write the expression that matches that description.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 3 columns of 4. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in word mode with two blank slots: ___ columns of ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: Equation Builder changes to expression mode: ___ × ___. Word description 3 columns of 4 remains visible nearby. Same tile palette.

beats.text: Build the expression for 3 columns of 4.

params.description: Expression 3 × 4 appears in Equation Builder.

beats.text: Yes. 3 columns of 4 becomes 3 times 4. Your description matches your expression.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 3 × 4.
4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in expression mode with two blank slots: ___ × ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: 4×5 dot array appears.
Equation builder appears in expression mode. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 12, 15, 20.

beats.text: Look at this array. Let's describe it by rows first. How many rows do you see, and how many in each row? Build the expression.
Build the row expression for this array.
That's it. 4 times 5 — 4 rows with 5 in each row.

elements.description: 4×5 dot array.
Equation builder showing 4 × 5.
4×5 dot array. place_tile tool active on equation builder.
Equation builder in expression mode. Empty slots ready for tile placement.

params.description: Equation builder clears.
Columns highlight on the array.

beats.text: Same array. Now let's flip it — describe it by columns. How many columns, and how many in each column? Build that expression.
Build the column expression for this array.
Right. 5 times 4 — 5 columns with 4 in each. Same array, two ways to describe it.

elements.description: 4×5 dot array with columns highlighted.
Equation builder showing 5 × 4.
4×5 dot array with columns highlighted. place_tile tool active on equation builder.
Equation builder in expression mode. Empty slots ready for tile placement.

params.description: Ice cube tray array appears on left side. 2 rows of 6 ice cubes.
Dot array appears on right side. 2 rows of 6 dots.

beats.text: Check out these two pictures. On the left, an ice cube tray. On the right, dots. Same number of rows. Same number of columns. Whether you're looking at ice cubes or dots, it's the same array. 2 rows of 6.

params.description: Rows highlight simultaneously on both ice cube tray and dot array.
Rows highlight on dot array simultaneously with ice cube tray.
Columns highlight simultaneously on both ice cube tray and dot array.
Columns highlight on dot array simultaneously with ice cube tray.
Ice cube tray fades and is removed from screen.
Equation builder appears below dot array in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication sign between them. Tile palette displays: 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12.

beats.text: The math works the same way no matter what's filling the spots. Build the row expression.
Build the row expression for this array.

validator.description: Student placed 2 × 6, correct expression for row perspective

params.description: Tiles 2 and 6 lock into place. Expression shows 2 × 6.

beats.text: Right. 2 times 6. Ice cubes or dots, the math doesn't change.

elements.description: Dot array. 2 rows of 6 dots.
Equation builder showing confirmed expression 2 × 6.
Dot array. 2 rows of 6 dots.
Equation builder in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication sign. Tile palette available. Student can place tiles.

params.description: 3×4 dot array appears. Same array from section 1.1.
Equation builder appears in equation mode. Blank slots for factors and product. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14.

beats.text: You get to choose. You can describe this array by rows, or you can describe it by columns. Either way works. Pick one, then build the whole equation with the product.
First, pick one. Rows or columns?
Choose if you'd like to describe this array using ROWS or COLUMNS.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array. Student has chosen rows or columns path.
Equation builder in equation mode. Blank slots awaiting tiles.

params.description: Word-mode equation builder appears: blank rows of blank items. Tile palette active.

beats.text: Now use words to describe what you see, and build the equation to match.
Fill in the word equation and the number equation.
Nice. You went with rows. 3 rows of 4. That's 3 times 4 equals 12.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array.
Word equation builder: 3 rows of 4 items filled.
Number equation builder: 3 × 4 = 12 filled.

params.description: Word-mode equation builder appears: blank columns of blank items. Tile palette active.

beats.text: Now describe it in words and build the equation.
Fill in the word equation and the number equation.
You chose columns. 4 columns of 3. That's 4 times 3 equals 12.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array.
Word equation builder: 4 columns of 3 items filled.
Number equation builder: 4 × 3 = 12 filled.

params.description: Equation builder appears in word specification template format.

beats.text: You can describe the same array two ways -- by rows or by columns. Each way gives you a different expression. Now let's flip it. You're going to BUILD arrays. You tell the computer what to make, then find the answer.

elements.description: Equation builder in word specification template format.

params.description: Empty array build area. No rows or items visible yet.
Row builder panel appears at bottom. Row +/- buttons active. Items per Row +/- buttons dimmed.

beats.text: Now you get to build arrays. Let me show you how this works.

elements.description: Empty array build area.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: I want to make 3 rows of 5. I said rows first, so I'll build the rows first.

elements.description: Empty array build area.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: First row goes in.

params.description: Row + button highlights.
First empty row placeholder appears, a faint horizontal line indicating where the row will be. No dots yet.

elements.description: Array build area showing 1 empty row placeholder, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Second row.

params.description: Second empty row placeholder appears below the first.

elements.description: Array build area showing 2 empty row placeholders, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Third row. Three rows set up, but no dots yet. I need to add columns to fill them in.

params.description: Third empty row placeholder appears.
Row +/- buttons dim and disable. Items per Row +/- buttons activate.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 empty row placeholders, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: Now I add items to each row. Watch this. Each plus puts one dot in every row.

params.description: Items per Row + button highlights.
First column appears: one dot in each of the 3 rows, forming a vertical line at the left. 3 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 rows with 1 dot each. 3 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: One dot in each row. That's 3 dots total.

params.description: Second column appears. 2 dots in each row now. 6 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 rows with 2 dots each. 6 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: Two dots in each row. Six dots. Keep watching.

params.description: Third column appears. 3 dots in each row. 9 dots total.
Fourth column appears. 4 dots in each row. 12 dots total.
Fifth column appears. 5 dots in each row. 15 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: Three, four, five in each row. Three rows of 5. Fifteen dots. Done.

params.description: Items per Row +/- buttons dim and disable.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both Row +/- and Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Now I write the equation.

params.description: Equation builder appears in equation mode. Template shows blank times blank equals blank. Tile palette displays numbers 3, 4, 5, 8, 12, 15, 18, 20.
Guide places tiles 3, 5, 15 into the three slots in sequence.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both buttons dimmed.
Equation builder showing 3 x 5 = 15. All slots filled.

beats.text: Three rows of 5. Three times 5 equals 15. I planned it, built it, got my answer. Your turn now.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both buttons dimmed.
Equation builder showing 3 x 5 = 15. All slots filled.

params.description: Empty arrays build area appears.
Row builder panel appears at bottom. Row +/- active. Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Let's build an array with 4 rows of 6. Start by making the rows.
Build 4 rows of 6. Add the rows first.

validator.description: Student pressed Row + 4 times, 4 empty row placeholders visible

params.description: Row +/- dims. Items per Row +/- activates.

beats.text: That's 4 rows. Now fill in each row with 6 items.

elements.description: Empty arrays build area with 4 empty row placeholders visible.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active. Items per Row +/- dimmed. add_row tool active.

beats.text: Add 6 dots into every row.

validator.description: Student pressed Items per Row + 6 times, complete 4x6 dot array visible, 24 dots total

params.description: Items per Row +/- dims.

beats.text: There it is. 4 rows of 6.

elements.description: Complete 4x6 dot array visible. 24 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed. Items per Row +/- dimmed.

params.description: Row builder panel removes.
Equation Builder appears. Equation mode: blank x blank = blank. Tile palette: 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 20, 24, 28.

beats.text: Now let's write the equation that matches what you built.
Build the equation for 4 rows of 6.
Right. 4 times 6 equals 24. You built the array and solved for the product.

elements.description: Complete 4x6 dot array visible. 24 dots total.
Equation Builder. Equation mode. place_tile tool active. Palette: 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 20, 24, 28.

params.description: Arrays build area empty. Column builder active: Column +/- enabled, Items per Column +/- dimmed.
Column builder panel. Column +/- button active. Items per Column +/- button dimmed.

beats.text: Now try building by columns first. Make 2 columns of 5.
Build 2 columns of 5. Add the columns first.

validator.description: Student pressed Column + 2 times, 2 empty column placeholders visible

params.description: 2 empty column placeholders appear.
Column +/- button dims. Items per Column +/- button activates.

elements.description: 2 empty column placeholders visible. No dots yet.
Column builder panel. Column +/- dimmed. Items per Column +/- active.
Arrays build area empty. add_column tool active.
Column builder panel. Column +/- active. Items per Column +/- dimmed.

beats.text: 2 columns. Each time you add, you're putting one dot in every column.
Add 5 dots into each column.

validator.description: Student pressed Items per Column + 5 times, complete 5×2 dot array visible

params.description: Complete 5×2 dot array visible (10 dots total). Each column has 5 dots.
Items per Column +/- button dims.

beats.text: You got it. 2 columns of 5. You built it column by column.

elements.description: Complete 5×2 dot array visible. 2 columns, 5 dots per column, 10 dots total.
Column builder panel. Both buttons dimmed.
2 empty column placeholders visible. add_item_per_column tool active.
Column builder panel. Column +/- dimmed. Items per Column +/- active.

params.description: Column builder panel removed from screen.
Equation Builder appears in equation mode. Tile palette shows: 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 12.

beats.text: Now write the equation for what you just built.
Build the equation for 2 columns of 5.

params.description: Equation 2 × 5 = 10 confirms.

beats.text: Right. 2 times 5 equals 10. Building by columns works the same way -- the way you describe it matches the expression you write.

elements.description: Complete 5×2 dot array visible. 2 columns, 5 dots per column, 10 dots total.
Equation Builder showing confirmed equation: 2 × 5 = 10.
Complete 5×2 dot array visible. 2 columns, 5 dots per column, 10 dots total.
Equation Builder in equation mode. place_tile tool active. Palette shows: 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 12.

beats.text: Last challenge. I want an array with 20 dots total. You decide: rows first or columns first, pick your numbers, and find the equation. There's more than one way to get this right.

params.description: Two multiple choice options appear: Row and Column.

beats.text: Choose rows or columns to build an array with 20 dots.

validator.description: Student selected either Row or Column, any response advances

elements.description: Multiple choice options showing Row and Column.
Multiple choice options showing Row and Column.

params.description: Multiple choice options disappear.
Empty array grid appears.
Row builder panel appears with Row + and Items per Row + buttons active.
Column builder panel appears with Column + and Items per Column + buttons active.

beats.text: Build an array with exactly 20 dots.

validator.description: Student built array with exactly 20 dots using rows

params.description: Row builder panel disappears.
Column builder panel disappears.

elements.description: Completed array with 20 dots on screen.

beats.text: Build an array with exactly 20 dots.

validator.description: Student built array with exactly 20 dots using columns

params.description: Row builder panel disappears.
Column builder panel disappears.

elements.description: Completed array with 20 dots on screen.
Array grid in building mode.
Row builder panel with both buttons active.
Column builder panel with both buttons active.

params.description: Equation builder appears in word mode. Blank slots for groups and items.

beats.text: You built it. Now write the word description for what you made.
Describe your array using words.

validator.description: Student placed word description matching their array with product 20

elements.description: Completed array with 20 dots.
Equation builder in word mode with student's description filled in.
Completed array with 20 dots.
Equation builder in word mode with blank slots.

params.description: Equation builder appears in equation mode. Blank slots for factors and product.

beats.text: Now write the equation. What multiplication does your array show?
Build the equation for your array.

validator.description: Student placed equation matching their array and word description, product equals 20

beats.text: Right. 4 times 5 equals 20. You chose 4 rows of 5. That's one way to make 20.

elements.description: Completed array with 20 dots.
Equation builder in word mode with student's description.
Equation builder in equation mode with student's equation.
Completed array with 20 dots.
Equation builder in word mode with student's description.
Equation builder in equation mode with blank slots.

beats.text: You can describe arrays by rows or columns now. You can write expressions for both. And you can find the product. Let's see how you do with this.
